demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care (nursing home) facility; requires assistance with daily activities

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every time I dry my face with a towel, this lump on my nose bursts open and bleeds everywhere. Today it just won't stop. (Reported with the facility registered nurse present, who is holding gauze to the spot.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A lump on the side of the nose started oozing blood a few hours ago after the crust came off, it has soaked through several tissues and won't settle, and the patient is very distressed by it.

symptom_narrative.onset: a small shiny raised spot appeared on the right side of the nose about two years ago and has very slowly grown since
symptom_narrative.duration: growing slowly over roughly two years; started bleeding on and off over the last three months; today it has been oozing continuously for about four hours
symptom_narrative.character: a slowly enlarging shiny lump that developed a crusty centre; the crust came off on its own today and now there is a slow, steady oozing of blood from the middle
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: very upsetting and messy — it has soaked through several tissues and made the patient anxious
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on the outer edge of the right nostril / side of the nose
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant slow oozing today; before this it bled only briefly after being knocked or after drying the face with a towel
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: pressing on it with gauze slows it a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: drying the face with a towel or any minor knock to the lump makes it bleed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no pain and no itch from the lump, either now or before it started bleeding
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: several short-lived bleeds from the same lump over the past three months after minor trauma, each of which had settled until today
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: nursing-home resident who needs help with day-to-day activities; frail and anxious about the bleeding

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally frail and needs help with daily activities

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a low-dose aspirin, one 100 mg tablet every day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility with nursing staff on site; a registered nurse is present and helping during this contact

objective_data_offered.value: a shiny, raised, dome-shaped lump about the size of a small pea (roughly 1 cm) on the outer edge of the right nostril, with a small central crater/sore in the middle that is slowly oozing blood; the raised edge looks pearly and shiny and has fine tiny blood vessels running across it
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described from a video/photo view and the carer's description; not a clinician skin examination or dermoscopy

objective_data_offered.value: the blood is a slow, steady dark-red ooze rather than a fast spurt; it has soaked several tissues but is not squirting
frail but alert and talking, sitting up in a wheelchair, holding a blood-stained gauze to the nose

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: measured and relayed by the facility nurse on a ward cuff during this contact
facility fingertip oximeter reading relayed by the nurse
